N1.label: Franchise Value Unlocked Post-Litigation
N1.description: Cosette appeal succeeds, removing the A$21M semi-annual litigation drag. Cash flow normalises, market re-focuses on NEXTSTELLIS franchise value (20-year exclusive, 80% margins, growing prescriptions). Earn-out obligations decline A$65M per year. Convertible note refinanced at reasonable terms. Fair value: A$4.50–6.00 per share based on franchise DCF.
N1.plain_english: Cosette appeal succeeds, removing the A$21M semi-annual litigation drag. Cash flow normalises, market re-focuses on NEXTSTELLIS franchise value (20-year exclusive, 80% margins, growing prescriptions). Earn-out obligations decline A$65M per year. Convertible note refinanced at reasonable terms. Fair value: A$4.50–6.00 per share based on franchise DCF.
N1.what_to_watch: Cosette appeal hearing (June 2-3, 2026), FY26 annual results, convertible note refinancing.
N1.upside: Material re-rating toward franchise value if Cosette appeal succeeds.
N1.risk_plain: Monitor for change in probability weight post appeal ruling.

N2.label: Litigation Drains the Franchise
N2.description: Cosette appeal fails. Ongoing litigation costs of A$21M per half consume operating cash flow. Convertible note maturity in November 2027 forces a dilutive raise or asset sale. NEXTSTELLIS franchise value captured by creditors rather than equity holders. Fair value: A$1.00–1.80 per share.
N2.plain_english: Cosette appeal fails. Ongoing litigation costs of A$21M per half consume operating cash flow. Convertible note maturity in November 2027 forces a dilutive raise or asset sale. NEXTSTELLIS franchise value captured by creditors rather than equity holders. Fair value: A$1.00–1.80 per share.
N2.what_to_watch: Cosette appeal outcome, convertible note maturity management, cash receipts trend.
N2.risk_plain: Significant downside if litigation fails and capital structure cannot be refinanced.

N3.label: Acquisition of Franchise Assets
N3.description: A larger pharmaceutical company acquires Mayne Pharma for the NEXTSTELLIS franchise and Women's Health portfolio. The 20-year exclusive licence and established distribution make this an attractive bolt-on. Premium of 30–50% to current price. Previous failed takeover (Cosette) demonstrates strategic interest in the assets.
N3.plain_english: A larger pharmaceutical company acquires Mayne Pharma for the NEXTSTELLIS franchise and Women's Health portfolio. The 20-year exclusive licence and established distribution make this an attractive bolt-on. Premium of 30–50% to current price.
N3.what_to_watch: Strategic holder registration, M&A speculation, FIRB considerations.
N3.upside: Acquisition premium of 30–50% to current price.
N3.risk_plain: FIRB national security constraints limit foreign acquirer universe.

N4.label: NEXTSTELLIS Growth Stalls
N4.description: NEXTSTELLIS prescription growth plateaus as generic oral contraceptives maintain share. The differentiated E4 mechanism fails to command sustainable pricing premium. Women's Health margins compress. The franchise fails to grow into the valuation even post-litigation. Fair value: A$1.50–2.00.
N4.plain_english: NEXTSTELLIS prescription growth plateaus as generic oral contraceptives maintain share. The differentiated E4 mechanism fails to command sustainable pricing premium. Women's Health margins compress. Fair value: A$1.50–2.00.
N4.what_to_watch: NEXTSTELLIS prescription volume data (IQVIA), gross margin trends, competitive entry from Opill and telehealth.
N4.risk_plain: Significant downside if growth stalls and cash crisis is unresolved.

big_picture: Initiating coverage on Mayne Pharma (ASX: MYX) with a MONITOR stance at A$2.38. The operating business is sound (A$413M revenue, A$86M EBITDA, 80% Women's Health margins) but the failed Cosette acquisition has created a cash crisis. The Cosette appeal hearing on June 2–3, 2026 is the dominant catalyst resolving direction.

Litigation Drains the Franchise — Cosette appeal fails. Ongoing litigation costs of A$21M
Acquisition of Franchise Assets — A larger pharmaceutical company acquires Mayne Pharma
NEXTSTELLIS Growth Stalls — Prescription growth plateaus as generics maintain share
